evals.prompt: write the migration SQL for the file_access table
evals.expected_output: A complete CREATE TABLE SQL for file_access with correct columns (file_owner, file_admin, member_assistants, external_assistants, customer_uuid, subers, branches_access, view_for_customer), correct types, GIN indexes, FK constraints, and RLS policy using the 3-gate pattern. Should NOT include users_access as a stored column.

assertions.name: users_access_not_stored
assertions.description: users_access is NOT a stored column in the CREATE TABLE — it is virtual

assertions.name: three_gate_rls
assertions.description: RLS policy includes all three gates: branches_access, users_access (via view), and ancestor EXISTS check

assertions.name: gin_indexes
assertions.description: GIN indexes are created on branches_access and users_access

assertions.name: view_for_customer_flag
assertions.description: view_for_customer column is present as a boolean

evals.prompt: what roles can be on a file and how does the exclusion flag work?
evals.expected_output: Accurate explanation of all 5 roles (owner, admin, member assistants, external assistants, customer), the view_for_customer flag behaviour (when false: both customer AND external assistants excluded from users_access), and the subers opt-in layer.

assertions.name: all_five_roles_mentioned
assertions.description: Mentions all 5 role types: owner, admin, member assistants, external assistants, customer

assertions.name: exclusion_covers_both
assertions.description: Correctly states that view_for_customer=false excludes BOTH customer AND external assistants

assertions.name: owner_is_partner
assertions.description: States that owner must be a partner (is_partner=true)

evals.prompt: i need to add access to a file for an external lawyer from a firm we work with, walk me through how that works
evals.expected_output: Explains that external users must first be registered in the org_users table, then their UUID can be added to external_assistants on the file_access row. Should mention the view_for_customer flag must be true for them to appear in users_access. Should NOT suggest adding them to council_members.

assertions.name: org_users_mentioned
assertions.description: Mentions that the external user must first exist in org_users before being added

assertions.name: external_assistants_column
assertions.description: Directs to external_assistants column, not member_assistants or council_members

assertions.name: exclusion_flag_awareness
assertions.description: Notes that view_for_customer must be true for external access to be active
